title: Book Of Nonsense Limerick 83.
content:
There was an Old Man of the Coast,
    Who placidly sat on a post;
    But when it was cold,
    He relinquished his hold,
    And called for some hot buttered toast.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Edward Lear, titled "Book Of Nonsense Limerick 83.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
